channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The world begins welcoming 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — From Sydney to Vladivostok to Mumbai, communities around the world began welcoming 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany's Scholz takes veiled swipe at Musk in New Year's address
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz offered subtle criticism around billionaire Elon Musk's influence on President-elect Trump early Tuesday, saying the nation's fate will not be decided by "the owners of social media.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: A new year dawns on a Middle East torn by conflict and change
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — In Damascus, the streets were buzzing with excitement Tuesday as Syrians welcomed in a new year that seemed to many to bring a promise of a brighter future after the unexpected fall of Bashar Assad’s government weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, WAFAA SHURAFA and LUJAIN JO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Lemon calls Trump supporters 'f‑‑‑ing idiots' amid H-1B visa infighting
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon poked fun at the Republican Party's infighting over H-1B visas — a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse — calling supporters of President-elect Trump "dumb f‑‑‑ing idiots.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Longest-held Guantánamo Bay detainee sent back to Tunisia
item.description: A detainee at a military prison in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, has been repatriated to Tunisia, the Pentagon announced Monday. Ridah Bin Saleh al-Yazidi, 59, was eligible to transfer following an interagency review process, the release said. He was the longest-held detainee at the prison.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for impeached South Korean president as political crisis deepens
item.description: A South Korean court approved arrest warrants on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence amid a criminal investigation into his martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Initial results of imprecise census in Myanmar show a slight population drop
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar’s military government announced Tuesday that provisional results of a census taken in October put the Southeast Asian nation’s population at about 51.3 million, a slight drop from the last official nationwide count of about 51.5 million taken 10 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel’s attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zimbabwe's new currency woes hit traditional stores while illegal night bazaars flourish
item.description: People in Zimbabwe are shunning traditional grocery stores to do their shopping at informal and illegal markets that pop up at night to avoid police raids.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Court issues arrest warrant for South Korea's President Yoon
item.description: A South Korean court issues arrest warrant for sitting President Yoon Suk Yeol over his decision in December to impose martial law over the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Guard soldier dies days after Christmas in 'non-combat' related incident in Iraq
item.description: An Indiana National Guard soldier deployed to Iraq in support of Operation Inherent Resolve died Saturday in a non-combat incident, Defense Department officials announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Finnish investigators find an anchor drag mark on the Baltic seabed after suspicious cable damage
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Finnish investigators probing the damage to a Baltic Sea power cable and several data cables said they found an anchor drag mark on the seabed, apparently from a Russia-linked vessel that has already been seized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea orders emergency safety probe of airline operations after plane crash kills 179
item.description: South Korean acting President Choi Sang-mok ordered an emergency safety inspection of the country’s entire airline operation on Monday, a day after a fiery Jeju Air crash killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu completes prostate surgery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent a successful prostate removal surgery Sunday, officials said. Netanyahu, 75, has had a series of health issues in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US investigators to assist South Korea with plane crash investigation
item.description: U.S. investigators are helping South Korea investigate the plane crash on Sunday that killed 179 people on board a plane from Thailand. The team of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
